title: seriphidium tridentatum subsp. wyomingense
intro: Seriphidium tridentatum subsp. wyomingense, a subspecies of the plant commonly known as Bigelow sage or Trident Sage, is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Wyoming and surrounding areas. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of essential oils and other bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Seriphidium tridentatum subsp. wyomingense include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and infusing the leaves in hot water, or using the essential oil extracted from the plant in aromatherapy applications.
